mnemonics: C.H.I.P.S. for Success: Clear Goals, Happy Client, In Budget, On Schedule, Project Scope met.
keyPoints: Project success is often defined by meeting the iron triangle constraints of scope, time, and cost.
Failure is frequently caused by poor requirements gathering, unrealistic scheduling, and inadequate risk management.
Stakeholder satisfaction and delivering business value are more holistic measures of success than just technical completion.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on the technical delivery while neglecting user adoption and business objectives.
Failing to properly manage scope creep through a formal change control process.
Not re-baselining the project plan after significant changes or setbacks.
